Title: NEET UG 2026 Re-Exam on June 21: Dates & Admit Card
Author: Vidyarthi Mitra News Service
Summary: The cancelled NEET UG 2026 exam is being held again on June 21, 2026. Here's everything aspirants and parents need to know - admit card dates, the new exam timing, fee refund details.
Content:
The cancellation of the NEET UG 2026 examination, which was scheduled for May 3 and subsequently cancelled by the NTA on May 12 due to a paper leak and irregularities currently being investigated by the CBI, has left over 22 lakh students in limbo; however there is a clearly defined path going forward.

The NTA will conduct the Re-NEET UG 2026 on Sunday, June 21, 2026, in offline pen-and-paper mode from 2:00 PM to 5:15 PM (including OMR and attendance). No fresh registration, application, or fee is required, if you registered for NEET UG 2026, you're automatically enrolled. Your earlier centre details carry forward, and fresh admit cards will be released from June 14. The admit card shows your exam date and time but does not by itself guarantee entry.

For fee refunds, submit your bank details - account holder name, account number, IFSC code, and bank name, before the deadline of June 22, 2026 (11:50 pm) and only through the official portal, neet.nta.nic.in.
Tags: NEET UG 2026, NEET Re-Exam, NEET 2026 Admit Card, NTA, Medical Entrance Exam, NEET Exam Date, NEET Preparation, MBBS Admission, June 21 NEET, Exam Tips

Title: JoSAA Counselling 2026 Schedule: Key Dates & Rounds
Author: Vidyarthi Mitra News Service
Summary: JoSAA Counselling 2026 is underway, with registration open until June 11 and Round 1 seat allotment on June 13. Here's the complete round-wise schedule, crucial deadlines, and practical tips to help you lock in your dream IIT, NIT, or IIIT seat.
Content:
All the information you need for JoSAA Counselling 2026 is here, including an overview of registration dates, deadlines to help you secure your IIT, NIT or IIIT seat.

Get ready for your journey to the IITs and NITs!
If you took the JEE Main or the JEE Advanced exams, your next destination is JoSAA! You can find both the IITs and there are many other types of universities. JoSAA is the Joint Seat Allocation Authority which provides the centralised method of allocating seats for institutes such as IITs, NITs, IIITs and GFTIs. Therefore if you don't know when to fill in your choices, you won't get placed, no matter how well you performed!

Key Dates for JoSAA 2026 (dates may change!)
The first day of choice filling and registration was 5 PM, June 2, 2026, and the last day to do so will be June 11, 2026. The first two mock allotment results (June 8, 2026 and June 10, 2026) will show where your rank would land should you qualify based on the mocks.
Round 1 allotment date is June 13, 2026.
Round 2 allotment date is June 30, 2026.
Round 3 allotment date is July 6, 2026.
Round 4 allotment date is July 10, 2026.
Round 5 (Final Round for IITs/IISc) allotment date July 16, 2026.
You must decide before the end of each round if you want to keep one of the three available seat types: Freeze, Float or Surrender. The only option available to you once you have moved from Float to Freeze is to continue moving from Freeze to Surrender.

What Happens in Each Round:
Every round follows the same four steps: seat allotment, online seat acceptance, document verification, and payment of the seat acceptance fee (₹35,000 for General/OBC-NCL/EWS and ₹15,000 for SC/ST/PwD candidates). The fee is later adjusted against your admission fee.
Miss a seat acceptance deadline and your candidature can be cancelled with no reinstatement, so track every date on josaa.nic.in carefully.
Tags: JoSAA 2026, JoSAA Counselling, JEE Main 2026, JEE Advanced 2026, IIT Admission, NIT Admission, Engineering Counselling, Seat Allotment, JoSAA Schedule, Choice Filling

Title: CUET UG 2026 Fresh Admit Card Released: What Affected Students Must Know Before June 6 & 7 Re-Exam
Author: Vidyarthi Mitra News Service
Summary: If your CUET UG 2026 exam was disrupted by the May 30 technical glitch and you left your centre, NTA has released a fresh admit card for your rescheduled re-exam on June 6 and 7, 2026. Here's everything you need to know about downloading your updated admit card and what to check before exam day.
Content:
On the morning of May 30, 2026, a technical malfunction by TCS iON, the NTA's technology partner, delayed thousands of CUET UG candidates by two or more hours during the first shift. Although 95% waited for the system to be restored, 3,765 candidates left before the exam resumed.

NTA's Resolution: Students who completed biometric registration that day but left before the restart will get a one-time retest on June 6–7, 2026. Updated admit cards were released on June 1 via the NTA portal. The original admit card is now invalid—only the retest admit card is accepted at the centre.

To access your updated New CUET UG 2026 Admit Card, follow these five easy steps:
Step 1: Go to cuet.nta.nic.in (the official CUET website).
Step 2: On the Homepage, locate and click on the 'CUET UG 2026 Admit Card' link.
Step 3: Enter your Application Number and Date of Birth/password to log in with NTA.
Step 4: After successfully logging in to the official website as instructed, your updated Admit Card will appear on your screen.
Step 5: Save your CUET UG 2026 Admit Card and print it out as you will need a clear copy for the Test.

Verifying Your Updated Admit Card
Once you have downloaded and saved your Admit Card, please ensure all the information listed below matches your actual circumstances.
Test Day: Confirm your Test date is either June 6 or June 7, 2026 or whichever applies to your case.
Testing Location: If you were rescheduled to a different location than the one you initially identified, be sure to search for your Test Centre location on a map before attending.
Shift and Reporting Times: Review the Shift and Reporting times as they may be different from what they were on 30 May 2026.
Your Personal Details: Ensure your Name, Roll Number and Photo are correct.

If any of the above details do not match what is correct, please contact the NTA at their help desk immediately.
📞 011-40759000
📧 cuet-ug@nta.ac.in

What to Bring with You on the Day of the Test
1. Your CUET UG 2026 Hall Ticket printed out
2. One Valid Government Issued Photo ID (e.g., Aadhaar, Passport, Voter ID, PAN Card, or Driving Licence)
3. Self-Declaration (Undertaking) Form: Download it with your admit card and fill out beforehand, but sign it only in front of the invigilator at the center.
4. Two Passport Sized Photographs: Identical photo that you uploaded with your application.
5. A Transparent Ball Point Pen
6. PwD/PwBD Certificate (If applicable)
Tags: CUET UG 2026, CUET Admit Card 2026, CUET Re-Exam 2026, NTA CUET 2026, CUET Technical Glitch, CUET June 6 7 Exam, CUET Hall Ticket Download, NTA Re-Test 2026, CUET UG Rescheduled Exam, cuet.nta.nic.in

Title: NEET Going CBT in 2027: How to Prepare for Computer-Based Mode
Author: Vidyarthi Mitra News Service
Summary: In 2027, NEET-UG will switch from pen and paper to computer-based learning. Here are some adjustments for prospective candidates and how to start preparing early for the digital mode.
Content:
NEET-UG to go digital in 2027!

NTA'S NEET-UG exam changes to a computer-based test (CBT) in 2027 to address the 2026 NEET-UG leak and to improve exam security; must now be taken via computer rather than on paper using OMR sheets.

Differences Between CBT and OMR:
CBT: read the question on computer screen and select answers with a mouse.
OMR: read the question and then bubble the correct answer on an OMR sheet.
The syllabus, duration, and marking plan will remain the same between the two formats.

How to Prepare for the CBT NEET-UG Exam:
Take mock tests to get used to reading from a computer screen.
Take the mock tests on a laptop or desktop computer, not just on your phone.
Familiarize yourself with the electronic navigation system and review tools provided in the CBT format.

Reports indicate that the NTA may start doing multi-session/multi-stage testing on the NEET-UG exam.
Tags: NEET 2027, NEET CBT, NEET computer based test, NEET 2027 CBT mode, NEET online exam, NEET UG 2027, NEET exam pattern 2027, NEET digital exam, NTA NEET 2027

Title: College Admission: A Simple Guide to Stay Ahead and Secure Your Seat
Author: Vidyarthi Mitra News Service
Summary: Confused about college admissions in 2026? Here's a clear, student-friendly guide to entrance exams, eligibility, deadlines, and smart planning to land your dream college.
Content:
How To Prepare for College: Stay Ahead of the Game

Let's face it, College admissions can be overwhelming. You have entrance exams, eligibility rules, a bunch of counseling rounds to go to and a multitude of deadlines, all of which make it easy to feel like you are playing catch-up at best. Luckily, a little planning can help immensely, and you don't have to do it all at once.

Think About Why Before You Think About Where
Before you get lost in rankings, first, think about what you want to be doing. What areas are you excited about? What career do you see yourself enjoying in the future? Once that is established, picking the right classes and colleges will be much less daunting.

Have Your Basic Information Sorted Out Early
Most of the last-minute fiascos happen because of minor things adding up. When you plan ahead by:
Checking out eligibility requirements before you start applying, thus there will be no surprises
Having your documents gathered up ahead of time (your score transcripts, I.D. and pictures)
Keeping an accurate list of key dates for applications, exams and counseling sessions

Start Early and Stay Informed
There isn't a shortcut to success. The most successful students didn't have the best luck, they had just started early and taken action when necessary.

Stay connected for the latest admission updates and career guidance.

Title: Top Universities in Maharashtra 2026: Best Colleges, Courses & Admission Guide
Author: Vidyarthi Mitra News Service
Summary: Exploring colleges in Maharashtra? Here's a student-friendly guide to the state's top universities, popular courses, and how to choose the right fit for your future.
Content:
Maharashtra is one of the best places in India to study at an excellent university. Unfortunately, it can be difficult to find the right university for you because they all have their pros and cons.

Which Universities Have the Best Academic Strengths?
Each of these universities has its own unique academic strengths and specializations:
IIT Bombay - Engineering, Technology & World-Class Research
ICT Mumbai - Leading School for Chemical Engineering and Pharmaceutical Studies
SPPU Pune - Strong School for Arts, Sciences, & Commerce
Symbiosis Pune AND VNIT Nagpur - Strong Managing Schools for Law & Engineering

Select Based on Course Vs. Brand Name:
While it's easy to get swept up in branding; keep in mind that the course selection is what really matters. So think about what interests you and where you see yourself working in the future and then locate that school and course combination.

Final Tip:
Before applying, check under admission process information for important items like eligibility, entrance exams, placement record. You'll save yourself some headaches by doing your research before applying! Follow us for information on a course-wise admission updates plus career advice!

Title: How to Choose the Right College for Your Career Goals
Author: Vidyarthi Mitra News Service
Summary: Confused about picking the right college? Here's a simple, student-friendly guide to choosing a college that truly matches your career goals.
Content:
Choosing a college isn't simply about getting a degree; it's about preparing to achieve the career you want. With so many choices that you have to make when it comes to colleges, the best way to start is by looking at your career-oriented goals instead of the name of the college you would like to attend.

Determine Your Career Goals
The first thing you should do is determine where you would like to be in a few years. Once you have established this direction, it will be much easier for you to choose the right degree program and the right college to attend.

Some Things to Consider
Before making a decision, you should consider the following:
1. Course relevance - Is the degree program related to the career you would like to have?
2. Placements - What is the university's graduate placement history? What are the average starting salaries of graduates?
3. Faculty & Facilities - It is important to have strong mentors and adequate facilities to excel as a student.
4. Fees and scholarship opportunities - Only choose a college that is financially feasible for you.

A Good Final Piece of Advice
Before making your final decision, speak to alumni, attend webinars, and visit the campuses of colleges you are considering. Having someone else's experience in evaluating a university is far better than looking at a brochure.
